ddls:
CREATE TABLE "players" (
  "player_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each player */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the player */,
  "abbr_name" TEXT /* Abbreviated name of the player */,
  "position" TEXT /* Position of the player on the team */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the player */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the player */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the player */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the player */,
  "height" REAL /* Height of the player in inches */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight of the player in pounds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("player_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the team is based */,
  "mascot" TEXT /* Mascot of the team */,
  "stadium" TEXT /* Name of the team's stadium */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Seating capacity of the stadium */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "player_teams" (
  "player_team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each player-team combination */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the player */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the team */,
  "season" INTEGER /* Season the player played for the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("player_team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_player_teams_player_id FOREIGN KEY ("player_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_player_teams_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "player_stats" (
  "player_stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each player-stat combination */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the player */,
  "season" INTEGER /* Season the statistics belong to */,
  "games_played" INTEGER /* Number of games played by the player */,
  "yards_rushed" INTEGER /* Total yards rushed by the player */,
  "yards_passed" INTEGER /* Total yards passed by the player */,
  "receptions" INTEGER /* Total receptions by the player */,
  "touchdowns" INTEGER /* Total touchdowns by the player */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("player_stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_player_stats_player_id FOREIGN KEY ("player_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g. coach, analyst, administrator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "user_access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-access combination */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the team */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the player */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g. read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_player_id FOREIGN KEY ("player_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "player_injuries" (
  "player_injury_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each player-injury combination */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the player */,
  "season" INTEGER /* Season the injury occurred in */,
  "injury_date" TEXT /* Date the injury occurred */,
  "injury_type" TEXT /* Type of injury */,
  "games_missed" INTEGER /* Number of games missed due to the injury */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("player_injury_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_player_injuries_player_id FOREIGN KEY ("player_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "player_awards" (
  "player_award_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each player-award combination */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the player */,
  "season" INTEGER /* Season the award was won */,
  "award_name" TEXT /* Name of the award */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("player_award_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_player_awards_player_id FOREIGN KEY ("player_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id")
)
